title: Mapo Tofu Vegetarian

seo.title: Vegetarian Mapo Tofu Recipe | Easy & Authentic Chinese Dish
seo.metaDescription: Craving authentic Chinese flavors? Try our easy Vegetarian Mapo Tofu recipe! Spicy, savory, and satisfying, this plant-based dish is perfect for any meal. Get the full recipe here!
longTailKeywords: how to make vegetarian mapo tofu
seo.h1: The Best Vegetarian Mapo Tofu Recipe
h2Headings: What is Mapo Tofu (and why make it vegetarian)?
Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Vegetarian Mapo Tofu
Tips for the Perfect Vegetarian Mapo Tofu
Variations & Substitutions for Your Mapo Tofu
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of spicy and savory vegetarian mapo tofu served with rice, garnished with green onions.
seo.imageCaption: Our delicious vegetarian mapo tofu, a plant-based take on the classic Sichuan dish.

description: Experience the fiery kick and rich umami of this authentic Vegetarian Mapo Tofu, a plant-based twist on a beloved Sichuan classic. Silken tofu cubes are simmered in a deeply flavorful, spicy sauce, creating a dish that is both comforting and exhilarating. Perfect for a hearty meal, this recipe brings the vibrant tastes of China right to your kitchen.

ingredients.notes: mixed with 2 tbsp cold water to make a slurry

instructions.title: Prepare Tofu and Aromatics
instructions.text: Gently drain the silken tofu and carefully cut it into 2cm (approximately 3/4 inch) cubes. Mince the garlic and grate the fresh ginger. Chop the green onions for garnish. In a small bowl, prepare the cornstarch slurry by mixing cornstarch with cold water until smooth.

instructions.title: Toast Sichuan Peppercorns
instructions.text: Heat a wok or large pan over medium heat without any oil. Add the whole Sichuan peppercorns and dry toast them for 1-2 minutes until they become fragrant. Be careful not to burn them. Remove the peppercorns from the pan, crush them lightly using a mortar and pestle or the back of a spoon, and set aside.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: Add vegetable oil to the same wok over medium heat. Once the oil is hot, add the minced garlic and grated ginger. Stir-fry for about 30 seconds until they are fragrant but not browned.

instructions.title: Build the Flavor Base
instructions.text: Add the doubanjiang, fermented black beans, and dried chili flakes to the wok. Stir-fry continuously for 1-2 minutes until the oil turns a rich red color and the mixture becomes very fragrant. This step is crucial for developing the deep, complex flavors of Mapo Tofu.

instructions.title: Simmer the Sauce and Add Tofu
instructions.text: Pour in the vegetable broth, light soy sauce, dark soy sauce, and sugar. Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer, stirring to combine all the ingredients. Carefully add the tofu cubes to the simmering sauce, gently folding them in to avoid breaking. Let the tofu simmer in the sauce for 5-7 minutes, allowing it to absorb the flavors.

instructions.title: Thicken and Finish
instructions.text: Give the cornstarch slurry a quick stir again and slowly add it to the simmering sauce, stirring constantly until the sauce thickens to your desired consistency. Remove the wok from the heat. Stir in the crushed Sichuan peppercorns and, if using, a drizzle of sesame oil for an extra layer of aroma.

instructions.title: Serve Immediately
instructions.text: Transfer the Vegetarian Mapo Tofu into a serving bowl. Garnish generously with the chopped green onions. Serve hot and immediately with freshly steamed white rice for a complete and satisfying meal.

tips: To achieve the authentic numbing sensation (ma la), always toast your whole Sichuan peppercorns before crushing them. This intensifies their flavor and aroma.
While silken tofu is traditional for its delicate texture, you can use medium-firm tofu if you prefer a slightly firmer bite. Just be sure to press it lightly to remove excess water.
Mapo Tofu is best served immediately and piping hot with plain steamed white rice, which helps to balance the dish's spiciness. Leftovers can be stored in the fridge for up to 2 days.
article: Mapo Tofu Vegetarian is a beloved Chinese Vegetarian dish enjoyed across Chinese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
